title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
How to Simplify Income Tax Filing using an Income Tax Calculator?
Meta description: An income tax calculator is a useful online tool that can make it easier for you to file your income tax returns. Find out how.
Keywords: income tax calculator, filing for income tax, filing for income tax returns, types of direct tax
Introduction
Earlier, income tax filing used to be a practice that only experts and professionals could do. But now, with more information and tutorials being made available online, coupled with the government’s measures to make filings under  Income tax simpler, it is now possible to file your own income tax return (‘ITR’) online.
To make filing of ITR even easier, you can make use of an income tax calculator before you file your ITR. Wondering what this online tool is and how it can help you? Check out the details below.
How does an income tax calculator help file income tax returns?
An income tax calculator is a free online tool that helps you calculate your tax liability. Using this calculator is very easy. And it can also simplify tax filing for you. Here’s how.
You can find the right tax regime for yourself
There are two tax regimes that you can choose from, namely the ‘old tax regime’ and the ‘new tax regime’. The old tax regime comes with higher tax rates, but gives you a wider range of deductions and exemptions to make use of. The new tax regime has very few deductions you can claim, but offers lower tax rates.
An income tax calculator can help understand which of these two regimes may be more beneficial for you. You can use this calculator to compute your tax liability under the old tax regime and the new tax regime. This way, you can identify the option under which your tax liability is lower and accordingly opt for the suitable  one.
You can identify your tax liability on time
A major part of filing your tax returns involves settling your tax liabilities in the first place. Delayed payment of taxes comes with penalties . Further, without paying your tax dues, you will not be able to file your tax returns which will ultimately attract its own penalties.
Since an income tax calculator can be used free of charge, you can make use of this online tool to identify your tax liability on time and pay it by the due date. That way, you avoid the penalty for late payment or non-payment of tax, as well as the penalty for delayed filing of ITR.
You can file your tax returns accurately
An income tax calculator may also assist  you in filing your ITR accurately. In your ITR, you will need to enter your basic details as well as your income details. This includes your total earnings under the various heads of income and your total deductions. By using an online income tax calculator before you start filing your return, you can have a copy of the calculations to cross-check your data. This helps you avoid any errors when you are entering the relevant details in your ITR.
How to calculate income tax using an income tax calculator?
The exact steps involved may change slightly from one income tax calculator to another. But broadly speaking, here is how you can calculate your income tax using this online tool.
Enter the basic details such as the financial year and your age.
Then, fill in the income details under the five heads of income, namely salaried income, income from house property, business income, capital gains and income from other sources. Remember that you only need to fill in whatever is applicable to you.
Next, you will need to enter the details of any deductions that you can claim. Generally, the calculator specifies these elements and sections, so you can fill in the information accordingly.
The calculator will then compute your tax liability.
Conclusion
See how simple it is to use an income tax calculator? And the benefits it offers are so many. So, before you file your ITR this year, make sure you use this online tool to quickly check your tax calculations and ensure that you choose the right tax regime for yourself.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
